metadata.title: SPOT-OD v1.3.1 validation-selected edge-only residual-refinement ablation
metadata.description: SPOT-OD v1.3.1-validation-selected-residual-refine adds a validation-selected edge-only retained-candidate residual-refinement ablation. The --node-disagreement-features omit setting removes eight node disagreement aggregate fields, reducing node feature dimension from 30 to 22 while keeping edge feature dimension at 10. The model uses development validation seed minimum 53, 82 fit/training samples, 37 validation samples, and validation_loss checkpoint selection. In the edge-only attention residual-refine configuration, all non-development rows reach 390.317 m versus the best retained candidate at 459.591 m, a 15.073% gain; fresh source-generation seeds 151/157/163/167 reach 386.373 m versus 445.943 m, a 13.358% gain. Versus the matched edge-only local/no-message control, attention has 24.835% all-non-development and 45.329% fresh advantages. Versus the edge-only mean graph, attention has smaller 3.983% all-non-development and 5.548% fresh advantages. Evidence scope remains limited to retained-candidate compact-simulator evidence; graph-path isolation is only against the matched edge-only no-message local control. This release is not operational POD, not public precise-reference validation, not independent-machine reproduction, not a full raw/training/all-filter rerun, not standalone learned recursive filtering, not broad learned-OD validation, and not operational learned OD.
metadata.notes: This v1.3.1 release is not metadata-only. It adds the validation-selected edge-only retained-candidate residual-refinement ablation for tag v1.3.1-validation-selected-residual-refine. The --node-disagreement-features omit setting removes eight node disagreement aggregate fields, reducing node dimension from 30 to 22 while retaining edge dimension 10. Development model selection uses validation seed minimum 53, 82 fit/training samples, 37 validation samples, and validation_loss checkpoint selection. Edge-only attention residual-refine reaches 390.317 m across all non-development rows versus the best retained candidate at 459.591 m (15.073% gain) and versus the matched edge-only local/no-message control at 519.282 m (24.835% advantage). Fresh seeds 151/157/163/167 reach 386.373 m versus the best retained candidate at 445.943 m (13.358% gain) and versus the matched local control at 706.730 m (45.329% advantage). The edge-only mean graph is closer, with attention-vs-mean advantages of 3.983% all non-development and 5.548% fresh. The concept DOI remains 10.5281/zenodo.20768672. No DOI is asserted for v1.3.1 in this metadata before Zenodo import. DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20842573 is cited as the prior v1.3.0 version. Scope limits: retained-candidate compact-simulator evidence only; graph-path isolation only against the matched edge-only no-message local control; not operational POD, not public precise-reference validation, not independent-machine reproduction, not full raw/training/all-filter rerun, not standalone learned recursive filtering, not broad learned-OD validation, and not operational learned OD.

record.pending_note: Zenodo record and version DOI for v1.3.1 will be assigned only after the GitHub release is created and imported by Zenodo.
